description.undefined: Ontology developed as an exercise for a course at the Universitat Oberta de Catalunya (UOC). It models medical devices, incidents, risks, and corrective actions under MDR/IVDR regulations.

subject.value: Representació del coneixement PAC2 - Spring 2026 - UOC

abstract.value: This ontology models medical devices, incidents, risks, and corrective actions under European MDR/IVDR regulations. It captures the relationships between devices, manufacturers, risk classifications, and incident management processes, supporting semantic interoperability and structured data representation. The ontology was developed as part of the PAC2 assignment for the knowledge representation course at the UOC.

seeAlso.value: European Union. Regulation (EU) 2017/745 on medical devices (MDR). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regulation_(EU)_2017/745
European Union. Regulation (EU) 2017/746 on in vitro diagnostic medical devices (IVDR). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regulation_(EU)_2017/746

comment.undefined: A potential source of harm associated with the use or malfunction of a medical device.
An event involving a medical device that may lead to or has led to harm, malfunction or safety issues.
A classification indicating the level of regulatory risk associated with a device, typically based on safety and intended use.
The current state of a corrective action, indicating whether it is open, ongoing, or closed.
An organization responsible for designing, manufacturing or placing a medical device on the market.
A medical device regulated under MDR or IVDR, including implantable, active or diagnostic devices.
A classification that describes the functional or structural characteristics of a medical device, such as implantable or active.
A high-level classification of risks based on their origin, such as device-related or patient-related risks.
A regulatory classification that determines the legal framework applicable to a medical device, such as MDR or IVDR.
A classification indicating the potential impact or seriousness of a risk, such as minor, serious, or critical.
A classification of corrective actions according to their nature or purpose, such as recall, safety alert, or in-situ correction.
A classification indicating the likelihood of occurrence of a risk, typically expressed as low, medium, or high.
A more specific classification of risks within a category, describing the nature of the hazard, such as infection or mechanical failure.
A categorization of incidents based on their severity or nature, such as serious incident or near-miss.
An action taken to eliminate or reduce the risk associated with a medical device incident.
